content: Node.js daemon as background service 2025: macOS uses launchd with .plist files in ~/Library/LaunchAgents (user) or /Library/LaunchDaemons (system). Linux uses systemd unit files. PM2 provides cross-platform abstraction — pm2 startup generates platform-specific startup script (systemd/launchd). For a distributable daemon, the pattern is: install script that generates the .plist or .service file and registers it. node-pty requires native compilation so daemon must ship as a prebuilt binary or compile on install. pkg or nexe can bundle Node.js app as standalone executable but native addons (node-pty) require .node files alongside. Recommendation: ship as npm global package + pm2 ecosystem file for service registration.
